name: Italian Cheese-it Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. For the first layer, put ground beef, salt, pepper, and garlic in a skillet over moderate heat. Stir the mixture with a wooden spoon until it breaks into large chunks of meat as it cooks. Cook until well done, drain fat, and spread the mixture into a 2-quart casserole dish.
2. For the second layer, put water and 1 tsp salt in a 3-quart saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat, add chopped zucchini or summer squash, and cook for 5-8 minutes or until very tender. Drain well and mash the squash with a potato masher or electric mixer until it is thoroughly broken up.
3. Add green chiles, finely chopped onion, and butter to the mashed squash, then stir until everything is combined. Spread this mixture over the beef layer in the casserole dish and sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese on top. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
4. For the third layer, beat eggs in a medium-sized bowl until light. Fold in cottage cheese and chopped parsley. Spoon the egg mixture evenly over the squash layer.
5. Sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese (adding more cheddar if desired) over the casserole and place it on the center rack of the oven. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until the casserole is thoroughly heated and the top layer is set. Serve immediately.
